# Field Note Civic Labs

Field Note Civic Labs is a small independent consulting practice based in Vancouver, British Columbia.

## Summary

Field Note Civic Labs helps small teams use AI on real administrative work, on-site or remotely: records, documents, spreadsheets, email, and recurring review tasks.

The work usually starts with a recurring task the team already finds slow or hard to repeat. Work can happen on-site or remotely.

Field Note helps map the work, organize the material, decide where AI can help, and leave behind a simple process the team can run again.

## Who it helps

- Small teams with scattered records, recurring paperwork, or manual review tasks.
- Small business owners and independent professionals with document-heavy work.
- Community-minded organizations that want practical AI help without a large software project.
- People handling intake, review, policies, public records, or internal working knowledge.

## What Field Note does

- Choose a starting workflow and map the files, emails, decisions, people, dates, and review steps around it.
- Name the thing being tracked: a client file, invoice, issue, request, record, project, or deadline.
- Track status clearly: what arrived, what was reviewed, what needs a decision, and what source each answer came from.
- Make the work reusable with prompts, checklists, review notes, spreadsheets, small scripts, or lightweight tools where useful.

## Representative project areas

- Records Monitoring: watch public or internal records, filter out noise, and show what changed.
- Document Intake and Sorting: sort incoming files, preserve originals, flag uncertain items, and keep a record of what changed.
- Financial Statement and Tax Workflows: clean up recurring statement or CSV review, spot duplicates or exceptions, and produce a short review report.
- Small Strata Governance Tools: help small strata councils keep recurring governance work clearer. This is governance workflow support, not legal advice.
- AI Opportunity Review: turn a loose AI idea into a short practical brief before a team commits budget or time.

## Boundaries

Small strata governance tools are governance workflow support, not legal advice.

AI should support human review. It should not hide the basis for important decisions, replace source checking, or make sensitive work harder to verify.

Location: Vancouver, BC
